clusters.title: Nations reject Trump's call to help U.S. with Strait of Hormuz
clusters.neutral_headline: Who is answering Trump’s call for help with Strait of Hormuz
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, France24, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Nations reject Trump's call to help U.S. with Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Trump is calling on nations to assist the U.S. with reopening the Strait of Hormuz, but so far, many are declining. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has the latest.
items.content: President Trump is calling on nations to assist the U.S. with reopening the Strait of Hormuz, but so far, many are declining. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has the latest.

items.title: China overlooks Trump's demands for Hormuz strait, calls for all sides to de-escalate
items.standfirst: China on Monday called on all sides to halt military operations in the Strait of Hormuz to avoid further escalation. The remarks came after US President Donald Trump urged China and six other countries to send warships to secure the vital shipping route. Beijing however made no mention of military intervention. Its incentive to meet Trump's demands appears limited. FRANCE 24's August Hakansson reports.
items.content: China on Monday called on all sides to halt military operations in the Strait of Hormuz to avoid further escalation. The remarks came after US President Donald Trump urged China and six other countries to send warships to secure the vital shipping route. Beijing however made no mention of military intervention. Its incentive to meet Trump's demands appears limited. FRANCE 24's August Hakansson reports.

items.title: Who is answering Trump’s call for help with Strait of Hormuz?
items.standfirst: Trump has criticised the response to his call to help protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz during the war on Iran.
items.content: Trump has criticised the response to his call to help protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz during the war on Iran.

items.title: 'Near universal refusal' - European leaders reject Trump's demand for Strait of Hormuz mission
items.standfirst: NATO allies and other Western nations pushed back Monday on US President Donald Trump's demand that military alliance members help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the critical conduit for crude oil Iran has effectively closed. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating reports from Brussels
items.content: NATO allies and other Western nations pushed back Monday on US President Donald Trump's demand that military alliance members help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the critical conduit for crude oil Iran has effectively closed. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating reports from Brussels

items.title: Trump says Hormuz Strait help ‘on the way’ as allies reject military action
items.standfirst: US president refuses to identify which countries he says are responding to call for naval coalition to open trade route.
items.content: US president refuses to identify which countries he says are responding to call for naval coalition to open trade route.

items.title: Trump laments US allies' lack of 'enthusiasm' to help secure Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Monday criticised US allies on Monday for a lack of enthusiasm in helping unblock the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing war against Iran. US and Israeli strikes on Iran have jolted global energy markets, sending oil prices higher and raising gasoline costs for US consumers.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Monday criticised US allies on Monday for a lack of enthusiasm in helping unblock the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing war against Iran. US and Israeli strikes on Iran have jolted global energy markets, sending oil prices higher and raising gasoline costs for US consumers.

European leaders, Japan, Australia and several NATO…

items.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
items.standfirst: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.
items.content: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

items.title: US realising it 'needs help from allies even if all-powerful militarily'
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump criticized US allies on March 16 for their lukewarm response to his call to help protect shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite that, he did claim that some countries had agreed to help - but from what has been heard so far, this isn't true as FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani explains.
items.content: President Donald Trump criticized US allies on March 16 for their lukewarm response to his call to help protect shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite that, he did claim that some countries had agreed to help - but from what has been heard so far, this isn't true as FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani explains.

items.title: US allies 'know how to deal with Trump' amidst threats over Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump has demanded seven countries send ships to the Strait of Hormuz to secure toe crucial passagepoint for global oil flows. He threatened that refusal to help would mean a 'very bad' future for NATO. But US allies refuse to be dragged into the ongoing war in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
items.content: US President Donald Trump has demanded seven countries send ships to the Strait of Hormuz to secure toe crucial passagepoint for global oil flows. He threatened that refusal to help would mean a 'very bad' future for NATO. But US allies refuse to be dragged into the ongoing war in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

clusters.title: Cuba begins restoring power after energy grid collapses in nationwide blackout
clusters.neutral_headline: Cuba begins restoring power after energy grid collapses in nationwide blackout
bullet_summary: On Monday Cuba was plunged into an island-wide blackout affecting 11 million people after a "complete disconnection" of its electrical system, officials said, amid a worsening fuel shortage
Reported by CBS News, France24, NPR News

items.title: Cuba begins restoring power after energy grid collapses in nationwide blackout
items.standfirst: The latest blackout in Cuba comes over a week after another massive outage​ affected the island's west, leaving millions without power.
items.content: The latest blackout in Cuba comes over a week after another massive outage​ affected the island's west, leaving millions without power.

items.title: Cuba hit by massive islandwide blackout as power grid crisis deepens
items.standfirst: Cuba suffered another nationwide power outage on Monday after its electrical grid collapsed, the third major blackout in four months. The shutdown underscores the island’s worsening energy crisis, with ageing infrastructure, fuel shortages and economic turmoil leaving millions of people facing prolonged and repeated electricity cuts.
items.content: Cuba suffered another nationwide power outage on Monday after its electrical grid collapsed, the third major blackout in four months. The shutdown underscores the island’s worsening energy crisis, with ageing infrastructure, fuel shortages and economic turmoil leaving millions of people facing prolonged and repeated electricity cuts.

items.title: Cuba hit by island-wide blackout as energy crisis deepens

items.content: On Monday Cuba was plunged into an island-wide blackout affecting 11 million people after a "complete disconnection" of its electrical system, officials said, amid a worsening fuel shortage.(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)

items.title: Texas lawmakers spar over DHS funding as long lines loom at US airports
items.standfirst: John Cornyn and Greg Casar debate TSA agent pay outside Austin airport as partial shutdown enters second monthSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxRepublican senator John Cornyn and Democratic congressman Greg Casar of Texas squabbled outside Austin’s international airport on Monday over funding for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), as the shutdown of the agency enters its second month.Cornyn, the longtime Texas senator who is locked in a tough primary battle against attorney general Ken Paxton, went to Austin-Bergstrom international airport to bring Transportation Security Administration (TSA) employees lunch. As he pulled up outside the terminal, he encountered Casar, whose district includes Austin and whom a spokesperson said was there to catch a flight back to Washington DC. Continue reading...
items.content: John Cornyn and Greg Casar debate TSA agent pay outside Austin airport as partial shutdown enters second monthSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxRepublican senator John Cornyn and Democratic congressman Greg Casar of Texas squabbled outside Austin’s international airport on Monday over funding for the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), as the shutdown of the agency enters its second month.Cornyn, the longtime Texas senator who is locked in a tough primary battle against attorney general Ken Paxton, went to Austin-Bergstrom international airport to bring Transportation Security Administration (TSA) employees lunch. As he pulled up outside the terminal, he encountered Casar, whose district includes Austin and whom a spokesperson said was there to catch a flight back to Washington DC. Continue reading...

Reported by France24, Sky News World, Al Jazeera

items.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of killing more than 200 civilians in Kabul strike
items.standfirst: The Taliban government in Afghanistan on Monday accused Pakistan’s military of targeting a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul in airstrikes that they say killed 200 people and wounded several others. Pakistan rejected the accusation, saying it had targeted "military installations".
items.content: The Taliban government in Afghanistan on Monday accused Pakistan’s military of targeting a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul in airstrikes that they say killed 200 people and wounded several others. Pakistan rejected the accusation, saying it had targeted "military installations".

items.standfirst: US president on social media ‘thrilled’ that Brendan Carr reportedly looking into broadcasters’ licensesSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxDonald Trump reinforced comments made by Brendan Carr, the chair of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC), threatening the broadcast licenses of news organizations that report unfavorably on the war in Iran.In a Truth Social post on Sunday night, Trump said he was “thrilled” that Carr was “looking at the licenses of some of these Corrupt and Highly Unpatriotic ‘News’ Organizations. items.title: Trump asks China if visit to Beijing can be delayed a month due to Iran war
items.standfirst: US president had earlier hinted trip could be put on hold if President Xi does not help unblock the strait of HormuzMiddle East crisis – live updatesTrump has asked to delay his planned visit to Beijing by about a month due to the Iran war, after earlier hinting he might put the trip off if his prospective hosts do not help to unblock the strait of Hormuz.The US president’s summit with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, was meant to take place at the end of March but Trump told reporters in the White House on Monday: “Because of the war I want to be here, I have to be here, I feel. And so we’ve requested that we delay it a month or so.” Continue reading...
items.content: US president had earlier hinted trip could be put on hold if President Xi does not help unblock the strait of HormuzMiddle East crisis – live updatesTrump has asked to delay his planned visit to Beijing by about a month due to the Iran war, after earlier hinting he might put the trip off if his prospective hosts do not help to unblock the strait of Hormuz.The US president’s summit with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, was meant to take place at the end of March but Trump told reporters in the White House on Monday: “Because of the war I want to be here, I have to be here, I feel. And so we’ve requested that we delay it a month or so.” Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says U.S. wants to delay his meeting with Xi in China by 'a month or so' due to Iran war

items.content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent cast doubt on the planned trip earlier, while insisting it was not meant to pressure China to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

clusters.title: French mayoral elections: Fractured vote reshapes alliances ahead of runoff
clusters.neutral_headline: French political parties seek alliances before final round of local elections
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: French mayoral elections: Fractured vote reshapes alliances ahead of runoff
items.standfirst: France’s first round of mayoral elections has exposed a deeply fragmented political landscape, with strong performances from both the hard-left La France Insoumise (LFI) and the far-right National Rally (RN) complicating traditional alliances. As candidates gear up for next Sunday’s runoffs, negotiations, withdrawals and refusals to team up are reshaping the electoral map across the country.
items.content: France’s first round of mayoral elections has exposed a deeply fragmented political landscape, with strong performances from both the hard-left La France Insoumise (LFI) and the far-right National Rally (RN) complicating traditional alliances. As candidates gear up for next Sunday’s runoffs, negotiations, withdrawals and refusals to team up are reshaping the electoral map across the country.

items.title: French political parties seek alliances before final round of local elections
items.standfirst: Candidates look for deals with rivals to boost chances as major seats including Paris, Marseille and Lyon appear tightPolitical parties in France are hastily attempting to negotiate strategic alliances before the final round of local elections this weekend, after a strong showing by the far right and the radical left.This Sunday’s final-round vote for mayors and local councillors in major cities including Marseille, Lyon and Paris is expected to be close. Continue reading...
items.content: Candidates look for deals with rivals to boost chances as major seats including Paris, Marseille and Lyon appear tightPolitical parties in France are hastily attempting to negotiate strategic alliances before the final round of local elections this weekend, after a strong showing by the far right and the radical left.This Sunday’s final-round vote for mayors and local councillors in major cities including Marseille, Lyon and Paris is expected to be close. Continue reading...

items.title: Negotiations in full swing in French cities over mayoral vote
items.standfirst: Following the first of two rounds of municipal elections on March 15, the race for many of France's bigger cities including Paris and Marseille looks set to be tight. Politicians are now moving to make cross-party alliances against they key opponents, after both the far right and left made inroads.
items.content: Following the first of two rounds of municipal elections on March 15, the race for many of France's bigger cities including Paris and Marseille looks set to be tight. Politicians are now moving to make cross-party alliances against they key opponents, after both the far right and left made inroads.

clusters.title: Ten ICU patients killed in hospital fire in eastern India
clusters.neutral_headline: Ten ICU patients killed in hospital fire in eastern India
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, BBC World

items.title: Ten ICU patients killed in hospital fire in eastern India
items.standfirst: Emergency responders carried patients out of a government hospital in Odisha, India.
items.content: Emergency responders carried patients out of a government hospital in Odisha, India.

items.title: Ten killed in fire at India hospital intensive care unit
items.standfirst: All the victims of the fire in Odisha state are patients, and 11 hospital staff are being treated for burns.
items.content: All the victims of the fire in Odisha state are patients, and 11 hospital staff are being treated for burns.

clusters.title: BBC files motion asking US court to throw out Trump’s $10bn lawsuit
clusters.neutral_headline: BBC files motion asking US court to throw out Trump’s $10bn lawsuit
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, BBC Top Stories

items.title: BBC files motion asking US court to throw out Trump’s $10bn lawsuit
items.standfirst: US president sued BBC over 'Trump: A Second Chance?', seeking $5bn for defamation, $5bn for unfair trade practices.
items.content: US president sued BBC over 'Trump: A Second Chance?', seeking $5bn for defamation, $5bn for unfair trade practices.

items.title: BBC urges court to dismiss Trump Panorama lawsuit
items.standfirst: The US president is suing for defamation over the way Panorama spliced together parts of a speech he made.
items.content: The US president is suing for defamation over the way Panorama spliced together parts of a speech he made.

clusters.title: Outrage as Afghan asylum seeker who fought alongside US dies in ICE custody
clusters.neutral_headline: Outrage as Afghan asylum seeker who fought alongside US dies in ICE custody
bullet_summary: advocacy group and a family friend said Sunday
Reported by Al Jazeera, NBC World

items.title: Outrage as Afghan asylum seeker who fought alongside US dies in ICE custody
items.standfirst: Family, advocates call for full probe after Mohommad Nazeer Paktyawal dies less than 24 hours after being detained.
items.content: Family, advocates call for full probe after Mohommad Nazeer Paktyawal dies less than 24 hours after being detained.

items.title: Afghan asylum-seeker dies after less than 24 hours in ICE custody
items.standfirst: An Afghan immigrant who worked with the U.S. military in Afghanistan died less than 24 hours after being detained in Texas by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, a U.S. advocacy group and a family friend said Sunday
items.content: An Afghan immigrant who worked with the U.S. military in Afghanistan died less than 24 hours after being detained in Texas by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, a U.S. advocacy group and a family friend said Sunday
